title: Axonopus capillaris
taxonomy: Axonopus capillaris is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae, commonly known as the signal grass or signal centipedegrass. It belongs to the genus Axonopus, which includes several species of tropical and subtropical grasses. This species is native to the Americas, with a wide distribution across Central and South America. Axonopus capillaris is often used as a lawn grass in tropical and subtropical regions due to its ability to tolerate shade and drought. The plant is also used as a forage crop and for erosion control.
uses: Axonopus capillaris, a tropical grass species, has been traditionally used in various folk remedies. Its medicinal uses include treating fever, inflammation, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Additionally, its extracts have been studied for their potential antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. More research is needed to fully understand and confirm the medicinal applications of Axonopus capillaris.

intro: Axonopus capillaris, also known as Capim-branco or white bristle grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the Americas. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which offer potential health benefits such as reducing pain and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Axonopus capillaris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Axonopus capillaris is often prepared as a decoction or tea, or used to make infusions, tinctures, and extracts for treating various health conditions.
